questions: You're someone who responds to good fortune with grace and generosity, never savoring others' misfortune. Your office rival, who publicly took credit for your work last month, just got passed over for the promotion in front of the whole team. How do you react?

You're someone who wins humbly and never takes pleasure in another's loss. You just found out that the person who outbid you on your dream house had their financing fall through, and the seller is coming back to you. What's your reaction?

You're someone who keeps a dignified response to fortunate turns of events. Your ex, who dumped you saying you'd never amount to anything, just saw your photo in a magazine feature about rising stars in your field. How do you feel?

You're someone who treats others' setbacks with sympathy rather than satisfaction. The colleague who constantly undermined your ideas in meetings just presented their own project and it was torn apart by the board. What do you think?

You're someone who stays gracious when things go your way, especially at others' expense. You pulled off a prank on your best friend that went perfectly — their face was priceless and everyone saw it. How do you feel about it?

You're someone who never gloats or relishes having the upper hand. In a heated family board game night, you just made a move that bankrupted your smuggest sibling who'd been trash-talking all evening. What's your reaction?

You're someone who takes the high road and doesn't enjoy schadenfreude. The professor who gave you a harsh and unfair grade just had their own paper rejected from the journal they bragged about submitting to. How do you react?

You're someone who responds to victories with quiet satisfaction, not impish delight. You bet your skeptical friend that your prediction about the election would be right, and the results just came in exactly as you called it. What do you say?

You're someone who stays composed and magnanimous even when you come out on top. Your neighbor who complained about your garden for years just got cited by the HOA for their own yard violations. How do you feel?

You're someone who never takes pleasure in getting away with something. You talked your way out of a speeding ticket with a completely made-up excuse, and the officer actually apologized to you for the trouble. What's going through your head?
